rationale: X engages more directly with the ground-truth threads: it explicitly discusses the trust_remote_code default flip (matches alex-jw-brooks's own bug note), confirms the deploy-override endpoint-restriction resolution now correctly follows the final pipeline (matches hsliuustc0106's major concern), and notes the switch to create_error_response (matches yuanheng-zhao's inline comment) — giving it broader recall of what reviewers actually flagged. Y also catches trust_remote_code and validates the resolution order, but misses the create_error_response point and the HF-config caching-mutability concern entirely. Y's findings (especially the missing try/except regression) are more rigorously line-anchored with quoted before/after evidence, giving it a slight precision/actionability edge, but one low-value finding (async_omni_engine naming) is unverifiable and minor. Neither review includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
